description: Added Appetite header field (time budget constraint) and three-tier implementation boundaries (Always/Ask First/Never) in Design Decisions. 15 assertions per eval (up from 13 in iter-6).

key_findings: Both new features (Appetite, Three-tier boundaries) adopted at 100% by with-skill specs (6/6 assertions pass). 0% by without-skill specs (0/6).
With-skill maintained quality on original 13 assertions (92.3% vs iter-6 95% — within noise, same borderline failures).
The delta widened from +51pp (iter-6) to +73pp (iter-7) as structural features accumulate.

Recurring borderline failure: no_implementation_leaks flags naming specific hooks (useState, useLatestRef) as implementation detail. This is a grader strictness issue, not a spec quality issue — naming modules is explicitly permitted by the skill.
alert-templates scored 100% (15/15) — the most complex eval (cross-module) produced the best result.
